title: rubus corymbosus subsp. inflexus
intro: Rubus corymbosus subsp. inflexus, also known as the western thimbleberry, is a subspecies of deciduous shrub belonging to the Rosaceae family, characterized by its edible fruits and medicinal properties. The plant is known for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, wounds, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Rubus corymbosus subsp. inflexus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health concerns.
